mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, discount rates and term-premium pressure ease, so equity valuation multiples expand and S&P 500 20-day forward returns turn positive. The signal is strongest when inflation is stable or falling, because rate cuts then reflect liquidity support rather than recession panic.
failure_condition: Reject if declining FEDFUNDS does not coincide with above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable to lower; specifically, if triggered months show negative average 20d returns or Rank IC stays below 0.03 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in PAYEMS signals stronger household income and final demand, which improves revenue expectations and risk appetite for cyclicals and the index as a whole. Higher payroll growth should therefore predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, provided job gains are not offset by a simultaneous unemployment spike.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS growth above the trigger does not produce positive mean 20d SPX returns, or if the signal works equally well when UNRATE is rising; falsify if the conditional hit rate falls below 55% with no improvement over unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor demand and deteriorating earnings momentum, which should pressure equity prices. The effect is directional over the next week because labor-market deterioration is quickly interpreted as slower consumption and higher recession odds.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp from its 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-triggered episodes do not produce negative average 5d SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in more than 60% of triggered cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO signals stronger industrial activity and better near-term earnings breadth, which supports cyclical stocks and lifts the index over a quarter. The mechanism fails when output gains are driven by inventory restocking without end-demand improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly INDPRO momentum does not predict positive 60d SPX returns, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03; also fail if the relation turns negative during periods of flat or falling industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in HOUST reflects housing demand and credit transmission improving, which boosts construction, durables, and consumer wealth effects. That combination should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month, especially when the housing move is not accompanied by faster inflation.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-triggered expansions fail to generate positive 20d SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL is accelerating; false if the average return under trigger is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated FEDFUNDS rate above neutral-tight levels increases financing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when rates are rising rather than already plateaued.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate regimes do not lead to negative 60d SPX returns, or if returns are positive in more than 55% of episodes when FEDFUNDS exceeds the threshold; also reject if the effect is absent in tightening months.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is rising while PAYEMS is also rising, sales growth is being matched by labor-income support, implying healthier end-demand and margin stability. This interaction should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, but not when sales growth occurs without payroll expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous RSAFS and PAYEMS growth does not outperform single-series conditions, or if the interaction term has no incremental predictive power over either proxy alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A negative gap between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth indicates output is weakening faster than sales, a classic late-cycle demand shortfall that should pressure equities over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest when GDP revisions confirm the slowdown rather than overturn it.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth falls below RSAFS growth by 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-under-sales slowdowns do not precede negative 60d SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in the majority of triggered observations; also fail if the relationship disappears once revisions are incorporated.
